political.s: FEC: $287K in executive political donations; partisan lean split across 15 executive donors.

labor.s: Via parent company Pepsi: 236 federal penalties totaling $77.9M since 2000 (Violation Tracker).

charity.s: $90M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.10% of revenue) via PepsiCo Foundation.

environment.s: Via parent company Pepsi: environmental violation: $3.6M in federal penalties (Violation Tracker).

privacy.s: Via parent company Pepsi: Terms-of-service privacy grade D (poor) via ToS;DR (tosdr.org), CC BY-SA 3.0 — transformed to TruNorth's scale.

wiki.extract:
Doritos () is an American brand of flavored tortilla chips produced by Frito-Lay, a wholly owned subsidiary of PepsiCo. The concept for Doritos originated at Disneyland at a restaurant managed by Frito-Lay.
In 1966, Doritos became the first tortilla chip available nationally in the United States. The initial flavor was simply toasted corn, followed by taco in 1967, and the now-ubiquitous nacho cheese in 1972. The chips are available worldwide in a wide variety of flavors, differing regionally.
Doritos has also gained notability for its marketing campaigns, including ads aired during Super Bowl

health.s: Via parent company Pepsi: 1 CPSC recall on record; sample hazard: a small wire can protrude through the fabric of the ears on these headbands.

news_items.title: 84% air 16% chips the truth about Doritos bags finally exposed - MSN
news_items.summary: An opinion/consumer article claims Doritos bags contain approximately 84% air and 16% chips by volume, based on an informal analysis.

recent_events.summary: An MSN article claims Doritos bags contain approximately 84% air and 16% chips, highlighting the ratio of slack-fill to product content.
